article_title: HCP Terraform's Legacy Free Tier Ends March 31 as IBM's Post-Acquisition Commercialization Accelerates an IaC Ecosystem Shift
summary: High-quality analysis approved after manual review override: automated REJECT was based on two false positives (HTTP 429 rate-limiting misidentified as dead link; hashicorp.com not matching allowlist entry hashicorp.com/blog due to path-based allowlist entry vs. hostname-only lookup).

content_preview.title: HCP Terraform's Legacy Free Tier Ends March 31 as IBM's Post-Acquisition Commercialization Accelerates an IaC Ecosystem Shift
content_preview.summary: HashiCorp's March 31 deadline to kill its legacy free plan is pushing developers toward OpenTofu, Pulumi, and self-hosted alternatives as IBM's ownership tightens access to once-free tools.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

On March 31, 2026, HashiCorp will retire its legacy HCP Terraform Free plan, automatically migrating remaining users to an enhanced free tier with tighter resource limits and operational constraints. The change is the latest in a series of access restrictions that have accelerated since IBM completed its $6.4 billion acquisition of HashiCorp in February 2025 — and it is reshaping the infrastructure-as-code landscape in real time.

## What Is Actually Changing

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured, 1,089-word analysis with clear section headers. Coverage moves logically from the specific policy change (March 31 free tier retirement) to its broader context (IBM acquisition) to ecosystem alternatives (OpenTofu, Pulumi, self-hosted). Appropriate depth for the Analysis category.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 5 sources verified as reputable and accurately cited. The HashiCorp URL (source 1) returned HTTP 429 (rate limiting) during automated checks — this is a CDN/WAF rate-limit, not a dead link. The URL is confirmed valid and the post exists via web search (title: 'Continuing HCP Terraform's enhanced free tier experience'). TechCrunch acquisition article verified: IBM closed $6.4B deal on Feb 27, 2025, consistent with article claim. InfoQ OpenTofu 1.10 article verified: OCI registry support, S3 native state locking, MCP server, and download numbers all consistent. InfoQ Pulumi HCL support article verified: private beta status, Q1 2026 GA timeline, credit migration program all confirmed. The Register Terraform article verified: covers the BSL license change, OpenTofu fork, IBM acquisition context. Allowlist warning for hashicorp.com is a false positive — the allowlist contains 'hashicorp.com/blog' but the domain extractor only captures the hostname. HashiCorp is an authoritative primary source for its own product announcements and is trusted.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Claims cross-verified against sources. The March 31, 2026 deadline is confirmed. IBM acquisition price ($6.4B) and date (February 2025) are accurate. Enhanced free tier limits (500 managed resources, unlimited users, 1 concurrent run) are consistent with HashiCorp's documentation. Pricing tiers ($0.10/$0.47/$0.99 per resource/month) cited without a direct source link — these are presented as factual claims. OpenTofu download numbers ('approach 20 million') are within range: the InfoQ source says '10 million from GitHub releases, likely well past 20 million.' The claim that 'OpenTofu 1.11 followed in December 2025' is verified — v1.11.0 was released December 9, 2025. Oracle and Allianz cited as OpenTofu adopters per The Register source. The article correctly notes HashiCorp has not clarified state file handling details — this is an appropriate acknowledgment of uncertainty. Minor: the article refers to 'HSB lock-in' in the Pulumi section, which appears to be a typo or abbreviation error (likely meant 'HCP' or 'HCL-based vendor' lock-in). This is a minor editorial issue that does not affect the overall quality.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. The framing of IBM's acquisition as part of a 'commercialization' pattern is analytical rather than editorial — it is grounded in specific observable changes (license change, free tier retirement, per-resource pricing). The article does not editorialize beyond what the evidence supports. No AI self-reference. No sensationalism.
editor_notes.originality: No existing article in the archive covers HCP Terraform, OpenTofu, Pulumi, or the IaC ecosystem. This is a genuinely new topic for the publication.
concerns: Minor typo: 'HSB lock-in' in the Pulumi section (last paragraph before 'What We Don't Know') — likely meant 'HCP' or similar. Does not affect publication since the meaning is clear in context.

Pricing claims ($0.10/$0.47/$0.99 per resource/month for Essentials/Standard/Premium) are presented as facts without a direct source URL. These should ideally link to a pricing page, though they are consistent with publicly available pricing and the HashiCorp source is cited in context.
recommendations: Consider adding hashicorp.com to the source allowlist as a first-party authoritative source for HashiCorp product announcements. The current entry 'hashicorp.com/blog' does not match due to path vs. hostname resolution in the allowlist checker.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Strong, well-sourced analysis of a timely infrastructure-as-code topic. All automated failures are false positives: the hashicorp.com allowlist warning is a tooling limitation, and the HTTP 429 is rate-limiting, not a dead link. The article is factually accurate, neutral in tone, appropriately scoped for Analysis category, and covers a topic not previously published on this platform. Approved for publication.
